text: Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth is exalting Allāh, the Sovereign, the Pure, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
tl: Yusabbihu lilaahi maa fis samaawaati Wa-maa fil ardil Malikil Quddoosil 'Azeezil Hakeem

text: It is He who has sent among the unlettered [Arabs] a Messenger from themselves reciting to them His verses and purifying them and teaching them the Book [i.e., the Qur’ān] and wisdom [i.e., the sunnah] - although they were before in clear error -
tl: Huwal lazee ba'asa fil ummiyyeena Rasoolam min-hum yatloo 'alaihim aayaatihee Wa-yuzakkeehim Wa-yu'allimuhumul Kitaaba wal-Hikmata Wa-in kaanoo min qablu lafee dalaalim mubeen

text: And [to] others of them who have not yet joined them. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
tl: Wa-aakhareena minhum lammaa yalhaqoo bihim Wa-huwal 'azeezul hakeem

text: That is the bounty of Allāh, which He gives to whom He wills, and Allāh is the possessor of great bounty.
tl: Zaalika fadlul laahi yu'teehi many yashaaa; wallaahu zul fadlil 'azeem

text: The example of those who were entrusted with the Torah and then did not take it on is like that of a donkey who carries volumes [of books]. Wretched is the example of the people who deny the signs of Allāh. And Allāh does not guide the wrongdoing people.
tl: Masalul lazeena hum-milut tawraata summa lam yahmiloonhaa kamasalil himaari yah-milu asfaaraa; bi'sa masalul qawmil lazeena kaazzaboo bi-aayaatil laah; wallaahu laa yahdil qawmaz zaalimeen

text: Say, \"O you who are Jews, if you claim that you are allies of Allāh, excluding the [other] people, then wish for death, if you should be truthful.\"
tl: Qul yaaa-ayyuhal lazeena haadoo in za'amtum annakum awliyaaa'u lilaahi min doonin naasi fatamannawul mawta in kuntum saadiqeen

text: But they will not wish for it, ever, because of what their hands have put forth. And Allāh is Knowing of the wrongdoers.
tl: Wa-laa yatamannaw-nahooo abadam bimaa qaddamat aydeehim; wallaahu 'aleemum biz-zaalimeen

text: Say, \"Indeed, the death from which you flee - indeed, it will meet you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, and He will inform you about what you used to do.\"
tl: Qul innal mawtal lazee tafirroona minhu fa-innahoo mulaaqeekum summa turaddoona ilaa 'Aalimil Ghaibi wash-shahaadati fa-yunabbi'ukum bimaa kuntum ta'maloon

text: O you who have believed, when [the adhān] is called for the prayer on the day of Jumuʿah [Friday], then proceed to the remembrance of Allāh and leave trade. That is better for you, if you only knew.
tl: Yaaa-ayyuhal lazeena aamanoo izaa noodiya lis-Salaati miny yawmil Jumu'ati fas'aw ilaa zikril laahi Wa-zarul bai'; zaalikum khayrul lakum in kuntum ta'lamoon

text: And when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allāh, and remember Allāh often that you may succeed.
tl: Fa-izaa qudiyatis Salaatu fantashiroo fil ardi wabtaghoo min fadlil laahi wazkurul laaha kaseeral la'allakum tuflihoon

text: But [on one occasion] when they saw a transaction or a diversion, [O Muḥammad], they rushed to it and left you standing. Say, \"What is with Allāh is better than diversion and than a transaction, and Allāh is the best of providers.\"
tl: Wa-izaa ra'aw tijaaratan aw lahwanin faddooo ilaihaa Wa-tarakooka qaaa'imaa; qul maa 'indal laahi khairum minal lahwi Wa-minat tijaarah; wallaahu khayrur raaziqeen
